name: Soil Science and Composting
tagline: Building fertile soil from organic waste, air, and patience — the biological engine behind reliable harvests
problem:
Dirt is not soil. Dirt is crushed rock with occasional luck; soil is a living community of bacteria, fungi, nematodes, and roots that store and exchange nutrients on a schedule humans only partly control. When people clear forest or plough prairie and harvest without returning organic matter, they mine the humus bank until yields collapse — not because NPK disappears entirely, but because water infiltration, cation exchange, and disease suppression degrade together.

Composting is the controlled acceleration of decay that would happen anyway on the forest floor, but stacked for human convenience and pathogen reduction. Done wrong, it breeds flies and rats; done right, it converts kitchen scraps, crop residues, and manure into stable humus that buffers pH and feeds the microbial loop feeding plants.

The measurement problem is literacy: most survivors can smell anaerobic slime versus sweet earth, but fewer can interpret a simple pH strip or a jar-settle test for texture class. Without rough measurement, people lime sandy soil that needed sulphur, or add nitrogen to already hot piles that needed carbon.

Climate matters: arid regions lose organic carbon quickly to oxidation; humid tropics leach nutrients if piles drain too freely. Any universal recipe is suspect — observation beats dogma.
overview:
Soil fertility rests on texture (sand, silt, clay proportions), structure (aggregates formed by roots and glues), chemistry (pH, cation exchange capacity), and biology (decomposers and mycorrhizae). Compost improves structure and slowly releases nutrients while inoculating beneficial organisms.

Hot composting mixes high-nitrogen greens (manure, fresh grass) with high-carbon browns (straw, sawdust) near a 25–30:1 C:N target for active thermophilic stacks. Turning introduces oxygen; internal temperatures above roughly 55 °C for multiple days reduces many human pathogens in manure while speeding breakdown — monitor with a long thermometer or a clean iron rod test.

Cold composting is slower but lower labour: alternating thin layers in a bin works if rodents are excluded and anaerobic pockets are avoided by coarse bulking agents like wood chips.

Green manures grow living mulch — legumes fix nitrogen symbiotically when inoculated with compatible rhizobia; tilling them in returns biomass. Cover crops also break pest cycles and reduce erosion.

Simple field tests: jar shake separates sand/silt/clay layers after settling; vinegar fizz suggests free carbonates; squeeze test maps plasticity. None replace occasional send-out samples if labs return.

On Mars, soil building is exobiology and chemistry — perchlorates and toxicity mean Earth compost lore applies only by analogy until ISRU washing protocols exist.

principles.name: Carbon to nitrogen ratio
principles.explanation: Microbes need balanced diet; too much nitrogen goes ammonia; too much carbon stalls heating.

principles.name: Aeration versus anaerobia
principles.explanation: Oxygen drives aerobic thermophiles; waterlogged piles go septic.

principles.name: Humus buffering
principles.explanation: Organic matter stabilises pH and water retention on sandy soils.

principles.name: Pathogen reduction
principles.explanation: Time-temperature profiles in hot compost reduce risks from manure inputs.

components.name: Tarp or straw cap
components.criticalNote: Remove if pile goes anaerobic slimy

components.name: pH strips and jar
components.criticalNote: Calibrate strips against known buffer yearly

rawMaterials.name: Nitrogen greens
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Inedible biomass from hydroponics trim; human waste only after dedicated safety processing — never raw in food compost
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Chop small; mix immediately with browns

rawMaterials.name: Carbon browns
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Screen out plastics; avoid painted wood

rawMaterials.name: Bulk agent
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Crushed foamed regolith simulants in research only
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Size mix to fist test — should crumble not ooze

buildSteps.title: Site bins on drainage with rodent defence
buildSteps.description: Two bays allow flip. Hardware cloth apron buried 30 cm outward stops rats tunneling.
buildSteps.warningNote: Trench cave-in — shore or step back width

buildSteps.title: Build first layer cake
buildSteps.description: Coarse sticks at base, then alternating thin green and brown layers until shoulder height.

buildSteps.title: Moisten to sponge wring test
buildSteps.description: Water evenly while building; squeeze handful — one drop, not stream.

buildSteps.title: Turn when core cools from peak
buildSteps.description: Record peak temp day; flip outer in when dropping; remix dry pockets.

buildSteps.title: Cure finished compost covered one month
buildSteps.description: Pathogens finish dying; mycelium stabilises humus; sieve and apply before planting.
buildSteps.warningNote: Dust — mask if windy and dry

history.event: Midden heaps accidentally improve adjacent garden soils.
Liebig and others formalise plant nutrition.
Industrial fertilisers complement but do not replace organic matter management.

impact:
Soil is the balance sheet of agriculture; compost is one honest way to credit the account.

Closed life-support systems treat organics as mass streams — same biology, tighter tolerances.

sources.title: U.S. EPA — Composting at home
sources.note: Municipal guidance adaptable to field practice
